items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026, and CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update addressing critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Part of APSB26-49, this vulnerability allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the affected application. It has a CVSS 3.1 base score of 7.5 and requires no user interaction.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique. While Adobe fixed it in the 2.4.9 pre-release branch as part of APSB25-94, no isolated patch exists for older supported production versions, and active exploitation has been observed.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities. These flaws could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and compromise store data.
